title: WtAgeRe ebspollock example
text: vignette shows RTMB workflow (ADMB toolchain required) using examples/ebspollock dataset, optional ADMB comparison .rep file already available.
code: library(WtAgeRe)  example_dir <- here::here("examples", "ebspollock") base <- file.path(example_dir, "wt") has_rtmb_api <- "fit_wt_rtmb" %in% getNamespaceExports("WtAgeRe")  # RTMB fit from wt.dat if (has_rtmb_api && requireNamespace("RTMB", quietly = TRUE) && file.exists(paste0(base, ".dat"))) {   fit_rtmb <- fit_wt_rtmb(     datfile = paste0(base, ".dat"),     control = list(iter.max = 200, eval.max = 400)   )   pred_rtmb <- fn_get_pred(fit = fit_rtmb, source = "RTMB")   fn_plot_anoms(pred_rtmb) } # Optional ADMB comparison (requires an existing wt.rep) if (file.exists(paste0(base, ".rep"))) {   pred_admb <- fn_get_pred(file = base, source = "ADMB")   fn_plot_anoms(pred_admb) } if (exists("pred_rtmb") && exists("pred_admb")) {   pred_both <- rbind(pred_admb, pred_rtmb)   fn_plot_anoms(pred_both) }

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: wt.tpl ADMB program estimates mean weights--age using mixed-effects growth model cohort year random effects. current form, model assumes observed age sampled fish true age — .e., ages measured without error bias. practice, however, otolith-based age readings can uncertain, systematically biased, simply unavailable survey years. ages misclassified, observed weights--age contaminated fish neighboring true-age classes, leading biased estimates growth parameters incorrect predictions future weights. document formalizes mathematical machinery needed extend wt.tpl accommodate age uncertainty, focusing : (1) baseline model structure, (2) age-reading error model, (3) induced likelihood, (4) bias-correction approaches.

previous_headings: Baseline Model (Ages Assumed Known)
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: deterministic backbone wt.tpl discrete, three-parameter von Bertalanffy weight function. Letting w‾\bar{w}_a denote mean weight true age aa, L1L_1, L2L_2 mean lengths youngest oldest modeled ages (a1a_1 aAa_A, respectively), =aA−a1+1A = a_A - a_1 + 1 ages total, mean length age : L‾=L1+(L2−L1)1−Ka−a11−KA−1,=a1,…,aA, \bar{L}_a = L_1 + (L_2 - L_1)\,\frac{1 - K^{\,- a_1}}{1 - K^{\,-1}}, \qquad = a_1, \ldots, a_A, corresponding mean weight follows isometric length–weight relationship: w‾=αL‾a3. \bar{w}_a = \alpha\,\bar{L}_a^{\,3}. growth increment age aa : Δa=w‾+1−w‾,=a1,…,aA−1. \Delta_a = \bar{w}_{+1} - \bar{w}_a, \qquad = a_1, \ldots, a_A - 1.

previous_headings: Baseline Model (Ages Assumed Known)
what: Dynamic Weight Predictions with Random Effects
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Let ŵy,\hat{w}_{y,} denote model-predicted weight year yy age aa. model propagates weights forward time using cohort (cc) year (yy) random effects: ŵy,a1=w‾a1exp⁡(12σc2+σccy), \hat{w}_{y,\,a_1} = \bar{w}_{a_1}\exp\!\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma_c^2 + \sigma_c\,c_y\right), ŵy,=ŵy−1,−1+Δa−1exp⁡(12σy2+σyuy),>a1, \hat{w}_{y,} = \hat{w}_{y-1,\,-1} + \Delta_{-1}\exp\!\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma_y^2 + \sigma_y\,u_y\right), \quad > a_1, : cy∼𝒩(0,1)c_y \sim \mathcal{N}(0,1) cohort random effect cohort entering year yy, uy∼𝒩(0,1)u_y \sim \mathcal{N}(0,1) year random effect governing common deviations growth increment, σc>0\sigma_c > 0 σy>0\sigma_y > 0 corresponding standard deviations. log-normal formulation (12σ2\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma^2 bias correction) ensures 𝔼[ŵy,]=w‾\mathbb{E}[\hat{w}_{y,}] = \bar{w}_a average.

previous_headings: Baseline Model (Ages Assumed Known)
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Observed mean weights wh,y,aobsw^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} dataset hh, year yy, age aa related model predictions : wh,y,aobs∼𝒩(δhŵy,,σh,y,a2,obs), w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} \;\sim\; \mathcal{N}\!\left(\delta_h\,\hat{w}_{y,},\; \sigma^{2,\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,}\right), δh\delta_h dataset-specific scale factor (δ1=1\delta_1 = 1 reference fishery dataset), σh,y,aobs\sigma^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} provided observation standard error. marginal negative log-likelihood (integrating random effects via Laplace approximation) : ℓ=∑h∑y∑(wh,y,aobs−δhŵy,)22σh,y,a2,obs+12∑ycy2+12∑yuy2. \ell = \sum_h\sum_y\sum_a \frac{\left(w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} - \delta_h\,\hat{w}_{y,}\right)^2}{2\,\sigma^{2,\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,}} + \frac{1}{2}\sum_y c_y^2 + \frac{1}{2}\sum_y u_y^2.

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Age uncertainty enters model least two distinct mechanisms.

previous_headings: Sources of Age Uncertainty
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Otolith-based age determination imperfect. fish true age *^* may assigned read age aa age reader probability: πa*,=Pr⁡(read age=∣true age=*). \pi_{^*,} = \Pr(\text{read age} = \mid \text{true age} = ^*). matrix 𝚷=[πa*,]\boldsymbol{\Pi} = [\pi_{^*,}] (dimensions ×AA \times , rows = true ages, columns = read ages) called age-reading error matrix (AREM). rows sum one: ∑aπa*,=1\sum_a \pi_{^*,} = 1 *^*. Entries 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi} typically parameterized discrete normal logistic-normal distribution centered true age spread σae\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}}: πa*,∝exp⁡(−(−*)22σae2),=a1,…,aA, \pi_{^*,} \;\propto\; \exp\!\left(-\frac{(- ^*)^2}{2\,\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}}^2}\right), \qquad = a_1,\ldots,a_A, normalized row sums one. parameter σae\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}} characterizes imprecision age reading; systematic bias can introduced shifting mode: πa*,∝exp⁡(−(−*−μae)22σae2), \pi_{^*,} \;\propto\; \exp\!\left(-\frac{(- ^* - \mu_{\mathrm{ae}})^2}{2\,\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}}^2}\right), μae\mu_{\mathrm{ae}} mean misclassification bias (negative = ages systematically underestimated, positive = overestimated).

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: years age readings collected, age composition weight sample unknown. true age distribution must reconstructed age-length key temporal smoothing, adding second layer uncertainty.

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: age-reading error present, actually observed year yy read age aa ŵy,\hat{w}_{y,} rather mixture true ages *^* misread aa: w̃y,=∑*=a1aAπa*,aŵy,*, \tilde{w}_{y,} = \sum_{^*=a_1}^{a_A} \pi_{^*,}\;\hat{w}_{y,^*}, assuming number fish sampled read age aa large enough treat w̃y,\tilde{w}_{y,} population expectation. quantity w̃y,\tilde{w}_{y,} expected contaminated weight — predicted value one observe ages recorded error structure encoded 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi}. matrix form, 𝐰y=(ŵy,a1,…,ŵy,aA)⊤\mathbf{w}_y = (\hat{w}_{y,a_1},\ldots,\hat{w}_{y,a_A})^\top column vector true predicted weights year yy, vector contaminated predicted weights : 𝐰̃y=𝚷⊤𝐰y. \tilde{\mathbf{w}}_y = \boldsymbol{\Pi}^\top \mathbf{w}_y.

previous_headings: Extended Likelihood Accounting for Age Error
what: Likelihood Conditional on True Age Assignments
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Suppose year yy read-age class aa, total ny,an_{y,} fish weighed, true ages distributed according probability vector 𝐩y,=(py,,a1,…,py,,aA)\mathbf{p}_{y,} = (p_{y,,a_1},\ldots,p_{y,,a_A}). observed mean weight read age aa year yy : wy,aobs=1ny,∑k=1ny,aWk, w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,} = \frac{1}{n_{y,}}\sum_{k=1}^{n_{y,}} W_k, WkW_k weight kk-th fish, true age ak*^*_k drawn 𝐩y,\mathbf{p}_{y,}. Conditioning vector true ages, mean variance wy,aobsw^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,} : 𝔼[wy,aobs]=∑*py,,*ŵy,*=w̃y,, \mathbb{E}\!\left[w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,}\right] = \sum_{^*} p_{y,,^*}\,\hat{w}_{y,^*} = \tilde{w}_{y,}, Var[wy,aobs]=σy,a2,obsny,+∑*py,,*(ŵy,*−w̃y,)2, \mathrm{Var}\!\left[w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,}\right] = \frac{\sigma^{2,\mathrm{obs}}_{y,}}{n_{y,}} + \sum_{^*} p_{y,,^*}\!\left(\hat{w}_{y,^*} - \tilde{w}_{y,}\right)^2, first term within-age-class sampling variance second term age-confusion variance arising spread true ages map read age aa.

previous_headings: Extended Likelihood Accounting for Age Error
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: updated negative log-likelihood, replacing ŵy,\hat{w}_{y,} w̃y,\tilde{w}_{y,} inflating variance include age-confusion variance, : ℓae=∑h∑y∑(wh,y,aobs−δhw̃h,y,)22Vh,y,+12∑ycy2+12∑yuy2, \ell^{\mathrm{ae}} = \sum_h\sum_y\sum_a \frac{\left(w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} - \delta_h\,\tilde{w}_{h,y,}\right)^2}{2\,V_{h,y,}} + \frac{1}{2}\sum_y c_y^2 + \frac{1}{2}\sum_y u_y^2, total variance : Vh,y,=σh,y,a2,obs+κh∑*πa*,(ŵh,y,*−w̃h,y,)2. V_{h,y,} = \sigma^{2,\mathrm{obs}}_{h,y,} + \kappa_h\sum_{^*} \pi_{^*,}\!\left(\hat{w}_{h,y,^*} - \tilde{w}_{h,y,}\right)^2. parameter κh≥0\kappa_h \geq 0 controls magnitude age-confusion contribution may estimated fixed based external information age-reading precision.

previous_headings: Extended Likelihood Accounting for Age Error
what: Marginal Likelihood over Uncertain True Ages
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: formal treatment marginalizes unknown true ages. Define probability fish weighed read age aa year yy true age *^* : qy,,*=πa*,afy,*∑″πa″,afy,″, q_{y,,^*} = \frac{\pi_{^*,}\,f_{y,^*}}{\sum_{''}\pi_{'',}\,f_{y,''}}, fy,*f_{y,^*} true age frequency (abundance--age) year yy, typically obtained external stock assessment estimated nuisance parameter. Bayes’ theorem, qy,,*q_{y,,^*} posterior probability fish read age aa true age *^*. marginal log-likelihood weight observation wy,aobsw^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,} : log⁡ℒy,=log⁡[∑*qy,,*ϕ(wy,aobs;ŵy,*,σy,a2,obs)], \log \mathcal{L}_{y,} = \log\!\left[\sum_{^*} q_{y,,^*}\;\phi\!\left(w^{\mathrm{obs}}_{y,};\;\hat{w}_{y,^*},\;\sigma^{2,\mathrm{obs}}_{y,}\right)\right], ϕ(⋅;μ,σ2)\phi(\cdot\,;\mu,\sigma^2) denotes normal probability density mean μ\mu variance σ2\sigma^2. integrates latent true age, treating candidate true age *^* mixture component weighted posterior probability qy,,*q_{y,,^*}.

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Let ‾*\bar{}^* mean true age fish sampled read age aa year yy: ‾y,*=∑*qy,,**. \bar{}^*_{y,} = \sum_{^*} q_{y,,^*}\,^*. age bias read age aa ,=−‾y,*b_{y,} = - \bar{}^*_{y,}. ,>0b_{y,} > 0, fish read age aa average younger reported (ages overestimated); ,<0b_{y,} < 0, older. resulting weight bias read age aa (relative true curve evaluated read age aa) approximately: Bias[w̃y,]≡w̃y,−ŵy,≈−,∂ŵy,∂|=‾y,*, \mathrm{Bias}\!\left[\tilde{w}_{y,}\right] \equiv \tilde{w}_{y,} - \hat{w}_{y,} \approx -\,b_{y,}\,\frac{\partial \hat{w}_{y,}}{\partial }\Bigg|_{\,=\,\bar{}^*_{y,}}, can approximated finite difference −,aΔa−1-\,b_{y,}\,\Delta_{-1} (using growth increment defined @sec-baseline). first-order approximation shows age bias causes weight bias proportional growth rate age; effect largest young, fast-growing fish.

previous_headings: Bias Characterization
what: Why True-to-Observed Ages Can Look Biased
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Even forward age-reading matrix 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi} weakly biased, inverse mapping read age expected true age often appears strongly biased practice. Three effects drive : Bayesian reweighting age structure. quantity relevant interpretation qy,,*∝πa*,afy,*q_{y,,^*} \propto \pi_{^*,} f_{y,^*}, πa*,\pi_{^*,} alone. older ages less abundant (common assessed stocks), posterior mass shifts toward younger true ages. Boundary truncation youngest/oldest modeled ages. age limits, probability mass fall outside modeled range folded inward normalization, creating asymmetric misclassification. Nonlinear weight--age. ŵy,\hat{w}_{y,} increasing curved age, symmetric age error age scale can still produce asymmetric bias weight scale. result, relationship read age mean true age can look systematically offset even age-reading error appears modest raw AREM.

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: many stock assessments, age uncertainty varies among years: years may high-quality age data dedicated otolith programs, others rely age-length keys age data . framework generalizes naturally allowing 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi} vary year: πa*,(y)=Pr⁡(read age=∣true age=*,year=y). \pi^{(y)}_{^*,} = \Pr(\text{read age} = \mid \text{true age} = ^*,\; \text{year} = y). years known, precise ages (σae→0\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}} \0), 𝚷(y)→𝐈\boldsymbol{\Pi}^{(y)} \\mathbf{} (identity matrix) model reduces original wt.tpl formulation. years ages uncertain, 𝚷(y)\boldsymbol{\Pi}^{(y)} spreads probability mass diagonal. years age data, one option set 𝚷(y)\boldsymbol{\Pi}^{(y)} broad prior (e.g., uniform adjacent age classes), remove year–age observations likelihood rely random effects borrow strength across years.

what: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: following R simulation uses parameters drawn wt.tpl (Atka mackerel configuration, ages 2–11) demonstrate practical consequences age uncertainty. Two scenarios contrasted side--side: Case 1 — age error: 𝚷=𝐈\boldsymbol{\Pi} = \mathbf{}. Ages known perfectly; simple fit von Bertalanffy mean weight recovers true parameters. Case 2 — Age error + bias: σae=1.2\sigma_{\mathrm{ae}} = 1.2 years, μae=−1\mu_{\mathrm{ae}} = -1 year (systematic underestimation). Contaminated weights systematically distorted, naïve fit misleads parameter estimation.

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: library(ggplot2) library(patchwork)   # for combining panels; install if needed  # ── Growth parameters (from wt.tpl initialization) ─────────────────────────── L1      <- 27.0          # mean length at youngest age (cm) L2      <- 46.0          # mean length at oldest age (cm) K       <- exp(-0.13)    # Brody growth coefficient alpha   <- exp(-11.0)    # length-weight scalar (gives weights in kg)  # ── Age and year ranges ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── age_st  <- 2L age_end <- 11L ages    <- seq(age_st, age_end) nages   <- length(ages)  styr    <- 1980L endyr   <- 2022L years   <- seq(styr, endyr) nyrs    <- length(years)  # ── Random-effects standard deviations ─────────────────────────────────────── sigma_coh <- exp(-1.8)   # cohort SD sigma_yr  <- exp(-0.82)  # year SD  # ── Observation noise (CV ~ 10%) ───────────────────────────────────────────── obs_cv <- 0.10  # ── Age-reading error parameters (Case 2) ──────────────────────────────────── sigma_ae <- 1.2    # imprecision: ~1.2-year SD in read ages mu_ae    <- -1.0   # bias: ages systematically underestimated by ~1 year  set.seed(2024)

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: # Discrete von Bertalanffy mean length then mean weight L_bar <- L1 + (L2 - L1) * (1 - K^(ages - age_st)) / (1 - K^(nages - 1)) w_bar <- alpha * L_bar^3          # mean weight at age (kg) wt_inc <- diff(w_bar)             # Δ_a = w_bar[a+1] - w_bar[a]  names(w_bar)  <- ages names(wt_inc) <- ages[-nages]

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Simulate True Weight Trajectories (with Random Effects)
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: # Draw random effects (unit normal, same convention as wt.tpl) coh_eff <- rnorm(nyrs) yr_eff  <- rnorm(nyrs)  # wt_true[year index, age index] wt_true <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nyrs, ncol = nages,                   dimnames = list(years, ages))  # First year: mean weights (no random effects to propagate) wt_true[1, ] <- w_bar  # Subsequent years: cohort effect enters at youngest age; # year effect scales the growth increment (exact wt.tpl logic) for (i in seq(2, nyrs)) {   wt_true[i, 1] <- w_bar[1] *     exp(0.5 * sigma_coh^2 + sigma_coh * coh_eff[i])   wt_true[i, -1] <- wt_true[i - 1, -nages] +     wt_inc * exp(0.5 * sigma_yr^2 + sigma_yr * yr_eff[i]) }

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Build the Age-Reading Error Matrix 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi}
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: #' Construct a row-stochastic AREM #' @param ages  integer vector of modeled ages #' @param sigma SD of the discrete-normal misclassification kernel #' @param mu    bias (shift) in read age relative to true age make_Pi <- function(ages, sigma = 0, mu = 0) {   A  <- length(ages)   Pi <- matrix(0, A, A, dimnames = list(true = ages, read = ages))   for (r in seq_len(A)) {           # row = true age     a_star <- ages[r]     log_p  <- -(ages - a_star - mu)^2 / (2 * sigma^2)     Pi[r, ] <- exp(log_p) / sum(exp(log_p))   }   Pi }  Pi_identity <- diag(nages)                           # Case 1: no error dimnames(Pi_identity) <- list(ages, ages) Pi_error    <- make_Pi(ages, sigma = sigma_ae, mu = mu_ae)  # Case 2

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: # For each year, apply Pi^T to the true weight vector: #   w_tilde[a] = sum_{a*} Pi[a*, a] * w_true[a*]  =  (Pi^T %*% w_true)[a] contaminate <- function(wt_mat, Pi) {   # wt_mat: [nyrs x nages]; returns same dimension   t(apply(wt_mat, 1, function(w) as.vector(crossprod(Pi, w)))) }  # Add independent Gaussian observation noise (proportional to true weight) add_noise <- function(wt_mat, cv) {   sd_mat <- wt_mat * cv   wt_mat + matrix(rnorm(length(wt_mat), 0, sd_mat),                   nrow = nrow(wt_mat)) }  wt_obs_c1 <- add_noise(contaminate(wt_true, Pi_identity), obs_cv)  # Case 1 wt_obs_c2 <- add_noise(contaminate(wt_true, Pi_error),    obs_cv)  # Case 2

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Fit Mean Weight-at-Age (Naïve VB Fit to Year-Averaged Observations)
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: # Objective: fit alpha, L1, L2, K to observed mean-weight-at-age profile # (ignoring year/cohort effects — this is the "plug-in mean" fit) vb_weights <- function(pars, ages, age_st, nages) {   L1_    <- exp(pars[1])   L2_    <- exp(pars[2])   K_     <- plogis(pars[3])          # keep K in (0,1)   alpha_ <- exp(pars[4])   L_a    <- L1_ + (L2_ - L1_) * (1 - K_^(ages - age_st)) / (1 - K_^(nages - 1))   alpha_ * L_a^3 }  nll_vb <- function(pars, obs_mean, obs_sd, ages, age_st, nages) {   pred <- vb_weights(pars, ages, age_st, nages)   sum(((obs_mean - pred) / obs_sd)^2) / 2 }  # Year-averaged observed weights and their SDs (across years) mn_c1  <- colMeans(wt_obs_c1, na.rm = TRUE) mn_c2  <- colMeans(wt_obs_c2, na.rm = TRUE) sd_c1  <- apply(wt_obs_c1, 2, sd) / sqrt(nyrs) sd_c2  <- apply(wt_obs_c2, 2, sd) / sqrt(nyrs)  # Starting values on transformed scale p0 <- c(log(L1), log(L2), qlogis(K), log(alpha))  fit_c1 <- optim(p0, nll_vb, obs_mean = mn_c1, obs_sd = sd_c1,                 ages = ages, age_st = age_st, nages = nages,                 method = "BFGS") fit_c2 <- optim(p0, nll_vb, obs_mean = mn_c2, obs_sd = sd_c2,                 ages = ages, age_st = age_st, nages = nages,                 method = "BFGS")  pred_true <- w_bar pred_c1   <- vb_weights(fit_c1$par, ages, age_st, nages) pred_c2   <- vb_weights(fit_c2$par, ages, age_st, nages)

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Age-reading error matrices two simulation scenarios. Left: identity matrix (Case 1 — error). Right: discrete-normal AREM σ_ae = 1.2 yr μ_ae = −1 yr (Case 2 — error + bias). cell shows probability fish true age (row) assigned read age (column). -diagonal mass leftward shift mode clearly visible.
code: Pi_to_df <- function(Pi, label) {   df <- as.data.frame(as.table(Pi))   colnames(df) <- c("true_age", "read_age", "prob")   df$true_age <- as.integer(as.character(df$true_age))   df$read_age <- as.integer(as.character(df$read_age))   df$scenario <- label   df }  df_Pi <- rbind(Pi_to_df(Pi_identity, "Case 1: No error (Π = I)"),                Pi_to_df(Pi_error,    "Case 2: σ_ae = 1.2, μ_ae = −1"))  ggplot(df_Pi, aes(x = read_age, y = true_age, fill = prob)) +   geom_tile(colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(prob > 0.005,                                sprintf("%.2f", prob), "")),             size = 2.5, colour = "white") +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Pr(read | true)",                        limits = c(0, 1)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = ages) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = ages) +   facet_wrap(~scenario) +   labs(x = "Read age", y = "True age") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),         legend.position = "right")

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Figure 2 — True vs Contaminated Mean Weight Profiles
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Year-averaged weight--age. Black line: true mean weight w̄_a. Blue points/line: Case 1 observed means (Pi = ) — recover truth within sampling noise. Red points/line: Case 2 contaminated means (σ_ae = 1.2, μ_ae = −1) — systematically elevated younger ages depressed oldest ages, reflecting mixing heavier older fish lighter younger fish respectively.
code: df_prof <- data.frame(   age    = rep(ages, 3),   weight = c(pred_true, mn_c1, mn_c2),   se     = c(rep(0, nages), sd_c1, sd_c2),   label  = rep(c("True  w̄_a",                  "Case 1: observed (no error)",                  "Case 2: observed (error + bias)"),                each = nages) ) df_prof$label <- factor(df_prof$label,                          levels = c("True  w̄_a",                                     "Case 1: observed (no error)",                                     "Case 2: observed (error + bias)"))  cols <- c("True  w̄_a"                       = "black",           "Case 1: observed (no error)"       = "#2166ac",           "Case 2: observed (error + bias)"   = "#d6604d")  ggplot(df_prof, aes(x = age, y = weight, colour = label, group = label)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = weight - 2 * se, ymax = weight + 2 * se,                   fill = label), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_colour_manual(values = cols) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = ages) +   labs(x = "Age", y = "Mean weight (kg)",        colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Figure 3 — Fitted Growth Curves vs Truth
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Naïve von Bertalanffy fits (lines) overlaid year-averaged observations (points ± 2 SE). Case 1 fit closely tracks true curve. Case 2 fit pulled toward contaminated data, yielding incorrect growth parameter estimates — curve steep young ages reaches asymptote quickly.
code: df_fit <- data.frame(   age    = rep(ages, 3),   weight = c(pred_true, pred_c1, pred_c2),   label  = rep(c("True  w̄_a",                  "Case 1: VB fit (no error)",                  "Case 2: VB fit (error + bias)"),                each = nages) ) df_fit$label <- factor(df_fit$label,                         levels = c("True  w̄_a",                                    "Case 1: VB fit (no error)",                                    "Case 2: VB fit (error + bias)"))  df_pts <- data.frame(   age    = rep(ages, 2),   weight = c(mn_c1, mn_c2),   se     = c(sd_c1, sd_c2),   label  = rep(c("Case 1: VB fit (no error)",                  "Case 2: VB fit (error + bias)"), each = nages) )  cols2 <- c("True  w̄_a"                      = "black",            "Case 1: VB fit (no error)"        = "#2166ac",            "Case 2: VB fit (error + bias)"    = "#d6604d")  ggplot() +   geom_errorbar(data = df_pts,                 aes(x = age, ymin = weight - 2 * se, ymax = weight + 2 * se,                     colour = label), width = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +   geom_point(data = df_pts, aes(x = age, y = weight, colour = label),              size = 2.5) +   geom_line(data = df_fit,  aes(x = age, y = weight, colour = label,                                 linetype = label), linewidth = 1.1) +   scale_colour_manual(values = cols2) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c("True  w̄_a" = "dashed",                                    "Case 1: VB fit (no error)"     = "solid",                                    "Case 2: VB fit (error + bias)" = "solid")) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = ages) +   labs(x = "Age", y = "Mean weight (kg)",        colour = NULL, linetype = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Figure 4 — Absolute and Relative Bias by Age
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Absolute bias (left) relative bias percentage (right) introduced age-reading error systematic underestimation age (μ_ae = −1 yr). Bias peaks intermediate ages growth increments Δ_a largest. oldest age mixture effect reverses sign older fish draw — truncation AREM age 11 pulls old-age weights slightly downward.
code: bias_abs <- mn_c2 - pred_true bias_rel <- 100 * bias_abs / pred_true  df_bias <- data.frame(   age      = ages,   abs_bias = bias_abs,   rel_bias = bias_rel )  p_abs <- ggplot(df_bias, aes(x = age, y = abs_bias,                               fill = ifelse(abs_bias > 0, "pos", "neg"))) +   geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(pos = "#d6604d", neg = "#4393c3"),                     guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = ages) +   labs(x = "Age", y = "Bias (kg)",        title = "Absolute weight bias") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)  p_rel <- ggplot(df_bias, aes(x = age, y = rel_bias,                               fill = ifelse(rel_bias > 0, "pos", "neg"))) +   geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "grey40") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(pos = "#d6604d", neg = "#4393c3"),                     guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = ages) +   labs(x = "Age", y = "Relative bias (%)",        title = "Relative weight bias") +   theme_bw(base_size = 11)  p_abs + p_rel

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
what: Figure 5 — Year-by-Year Weight Trajectories at Selected Ages
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: Annual weight trajectories four representative ages (3, 5, 8, 11) true process (black), Case 1 observations (blue), Case 2 contaminated observations (red). Case 1 tracks truth within observation noise. Case 2 persistently biased — magnitude shift matches static bias shown Figure 4, demonstrating age-reading error induces systematic, year-invariant offset rather additional random noise.
code: sel_ages <- c(3, 5, 8, 11) idx      <- match(sel_ages, ages)  df_traj <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(sel_ages), function(k) {   a  <- sel_ages[k]   ia <- idx[k]   data.frame(     year    = rep(years, 3),     weight  = c(wt_true[, ia], wt_obs_c1[, ia], wt_obs_c2[, ia]),     label   = rep(c("True", "Case 1 (no error)", "Case 2 (error + bias)"),                   each = nyrs),     age_lab = paste0("Age ", a)   ) })) df_traj$label   <- factor(df_traj$label,                             levels = c("True",                                        "Case 1 (no error)",                                        "Case 2 (error + bias)")) df_traj$age_lab <- factor(df_traj$age_lab,                            levels = paste0("Age ", sel_ages))  cols3 <- c("True"                   = "black",            "Case 1 (no error)"      = "#2166ac",            "Case 2 (error + bias)"  = "#d6604d")  ggplot(df_traj, aes(x = year, y = weight,                      colour = label, group = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +   facet_wrap(~age_lab, scales = "free_y", nrow = 2) +   scale_colour_manual(values = cols3) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Weight (kg)", colour = NULL) +   theme_bw(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom",         strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92"))

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
code: # Extract on natural scale extract_pars <- function(par) {   c(L1    = exp(par[1]),     L2    = exp(par[2]),     K     = plogis(par[3]),     alpha = exp(par[4])) }  tab <- rbind(True    = c(L1 = L1, L2 = L2, K = K, alpha = alpha),              "Case 1" = extract_pars(fit_c1$par),              "Case 2" = extract_pars(fit_c2$par))  knitr::kable(round(tab, 5),              col.names = c("L₁ (cm)", "L₂ (cm)", "K", "α"),              caption = NULL)

previous_headings: Simulation: Comparing Known-Age vs Age-Error Scenarios
title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: simulation makes three points concrete: Age-confusion bias largest fast-growing ages. Figure 4, peak absolute bias occurs around ages 4–6 growth increments Δa\Delta_a steepest. oldest age truncation 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi} model boundary reverses direction bias. bias systematic, random. Figure 5 shows Case 2 trajectories offset truth nearly constant amount across years. means year cohort random effects absorb distortion — random effects estimated correctly average, mean weight profile w‾\bar{w}_a wrong. Naïve growth parameter estimation compromised. parameter recovery table shows KK α\alpha noticeably distorted Case 2, even though sample size (43 years) substantial. distorted parameters propagate forecast weights (wt_cur, wt_next, wt_yraf) meaningfully affect stock assessment inputs.

title: Accounting for Age Uncertainty in Weights-at-Age Estimation
text: document establishes theoretical framework. Concrete next steps implementation include: Obtain simulate 𝚷\boldsymbol{\Pi} age-reading precision studies (e.g., double-reads, known-age fish). matrix year-specific wherever age-reading protocols changed. Test sensitivity — run model 𝚷=𝐈\boldsymbol{\Pi} = \mathbf{} confirm reproduces current output, introduce progressively wider error distributions. Compare likelihoods (AIC/BIC) base model age-error-corrected model assess whether data support additional complexity. Propagate uncertainty forecast weights (wt_cur, wt_next, wt_yraf) using delta method MCMC ensure age-reading uncertainty properly reflected standard errors reported sdreport.
